name: Modular Rack
description: While I did not create the metal racks that the modules are mounted in, I did build the housing for the metal racks out of wood with my faather. This was a gratifying project that turned out really well.

images.alt: In progress photo of the modular rack build, showing the wooden housing for the metal racks.
Another in progress photo, before the top of the housing was added.
Finished modular rack housing; the top row of of the rack is now nearly filled.
Another angle; to make the emblem on the side, I used a router to carve it into the wood then used a wood burning tool to darken the lines.
The other side of the modular rack housing, showing the emblem and the finished wood.
Top view; I used a sheet of clear acrylic as the backing. One day I'll mount leds that are voltage controlled by the synth itself.
In use on the top of the piano.

Short video of the modular rack in use. All sounds are generated by the modular synth itself, with no external processing. Midi is sent from a DAW to the synth to the speakers. Not incredibly impressively sounding but still fun to watch.

name: Modular Riffing
description: Sometimes it's just fun to daydream and come up with ideas. I wrote a couple (well, more than a couple...) down. Below are pictures of the breadboards of some of the concepts and notes I made of other ideas.

images.alt: Partial breadboard shot for the image-to-cv converter. The breadboard is a bit messy, but it works.
Another partial breadboard shot for the image-to-cv converter. The implementation of the concept included an SD card reader that read RGB values from an image
Adding some poteniometers to the image-to-cv converter to allow for more control over the output.
Notes, thoughts and ideas for other modules and projects. I like to write down ideas as they come to me, even if I don't have time to implement them right away.
Sketching a simple schematic/flow diagram for the image-to-cv converter.
Idea for a sampler/sequencer module that would allow for more complex patterns and sequences to be created.
A clock/clock divider idea. No idea what the hook was, I'd have to look back at my notes...
